task.title: Diagnose Slack threads read-down wedge (bare-vs-suffixed fs/events)

events.content: Root cause of threads not reading down: daemon literal-prefix isUnderRemoteRoot dropped bare-channel reply events; fix at the fs/events emit site (cloud#2010)

raw.question: Why do Slack thread replies never materialize under the suffixed mount root?
raw.chosen: fs/events must emit the SUFFIXED channel path; fix at the emit site (cloud#2010), verified live with no Pear restart
alternatives: treat as a v0.8.19 Pear regression
widen daemon isUnderRemoteRoot to also accept bare channel ids
raw.reasoning: fs/events emitted the BARE channel id while the mount root is suffixed <id>__<slug>. The daemon's literal-prefix isUnderRemoteRoot check rejected the bare-rooted reply events as outside the remote root, so replies were dropped before materializing. This is NOT a v0.8.19 Pear regression. Fix: emit the suffixed path at the event source (cloud#2010). Verified live in prod, no Pear restart needed. Required fast-follow: a channelName-absent no-bare fallback so events without a resolvable slug still route.

retrospective.summary: Resolved Slack thread read-down in prod via cloud#2010: fs/events were emitting the bare channel id against a suffixed mount root, so the daemon's literal-prefix filter dropped reply events. Fix emits the suffixed path at the source. Verified live, no restart. Not a Pear regression.
retrospective.approach: Trace the event from emit -> daemon filter -> mount; compare emitted path root against mount root; fix at the earliest layer that owns the canonical path.
